name: Wok-Seared Mahi Mahi W/Stir-Fried Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Fresh Ginger Root, peeled and minced
1/2 cup Red Bell Pepper, thinly sliced
1/2 cup Green Bell Pepper, thinly sliced
1/2 cup Yellow Bell Pepper, thinly sliced
1/2 cup Snow Peas, cut in half on bias
recipeInstructions:
For the Fish:
1. Season mahi mahi medallions with five-spice powder and salt.
2. In a hot wok, add peanut oil and sear fish on both sides. Take out and keep warm.
3. Wipe the wok (do not wash) with a kitchen towel.
4. Add peanut oil back to the wok. When smoking, add garlic, ginger, and green onions, stir.
5. Add peppers and carrots and cook for about 1 minute.
6. Next, add the snow peas and bean sprouts and stir well.
7. Spread the vegetables to the sides of the wok so that the center is empty.
8. Pour soy sauce and peanut butter into the center, stirring until peanut butter melts and is incorporated with soy sauce.
9. Stir with vegetables, then add sesame seeds and sesame oil.
10. Season with pepper, and set aside.
To Make Yucca "Hay":
1. In salted boiling water, parboil yuca for about 7 to 10 minutes. Remove from water and cool.
2. Peel yuca and slice into very thin strips.
3. In hot vegetable oil, fry yuca until golden; remove onto paper towels and season with salt.
To Assemble Dish:
1. In the center of the plate, spoon stir-fried vegetables.
2. Arrange the mahi mahi medallions around the vegetables.
3. Loosely pile yucca "hay" on top of fish.
4. Garnish with a sprinkling of fresh herbs.
Makes 4 servings
